recalls.Description: Kalencom Corporation has recalled its SARO Braided Crib Bumpers, Model 1374, available in salmon, gray, and mint colors. These crib bumpers, which have "Ref. 1374" marked on a sewn tag and packaging, pose a serious risk due to their potential to obstruct an infant's breathing, leading to suffocation. This product violates the federal Safe Sleep for Babies Act, which prohibits padded crib bumpers.
recalls.Title: Kalencom Corporation Issues Recall for SARO Braided Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can contact Kalencom Corporation at 800-344-6699 from 9 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kalencombaby.com/recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled SARO Braided Crib Bumpers in gray, mint and salmon
Location of product tags showing "Ref. 1374"

Retailers.Name: MacroBaby in Orlando, Florida, and online at Kalencombaby.com and Target.com from May 2024 through October 2025 for approximately $60.

Importers.Name: The Kalencom Corporation, of New Orleans, Louisiana

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstructed breathing, violating federal crib bumper regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the crib bumpers immediately and request a full refund. Those who bought the bumpers at MacroBaby in Orlando can return them for a refund. Online purchasers should email info@kalencom.com with the subject line 'Crib Bumper Recall - Refund Request,' including their name, phone number, and a photo of the crib bumper cut in half as proof of destruction.

recalls.metaDescription: Kalencom recalls SARO Braided Crib Bumpers due to suffocation risks. Immediate action recommended for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Little Pea Shop has issued a recall for two styles of its padded crib bumpers due to serious safety concerns. The "Waffle Bedding Accessory" style, available in gray, pink, blue, and white, was sold in lengths of 23.5 inches, 47 inches, 78.5 inches, and 94.5 inches. The "Plush Crib Bumper" style featured vertical crib slat covers with Velcro, designed in beige and ivory with bear, rabbit, or star patterns. These products pose a suffocation risk to infants, violating federal regulations.
recalls.Title: Recall of Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard; Federal Crib Bumper Ban Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Little Pea Shop via email at support@littlepeashop.com or visit https://littlepeashop.com/pages/recall. Additional details can be found on their website by clicking "Recall" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Little Pea Shop Waffle Bedding Accessory Crib Bumpers

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from September 2025 through October 2025, priced between $8 and $30.

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation due to obstruction of an infant's breathing, creating an unsafe sleeping environment.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the crib bumpers immediately and contact Little Pea Shop for a full refund. Cut the padding from the bumpers and email a photo of the destroyed items to support@littlepeashop.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Little Pea Shop recalls crib bumpers due to suffocation risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description:
LXDHSTRA has issued a recall for its baby loungers, which consist of a foam sleeping pad and padded bumpers with a cloth cover. These loungers were available in various designs, including tan star, big elephant, green elephant, gray feather, and gray rainbow, and were sold with a red and black padded crib bumper featuring letters "A" through "F" and animal characters. The products pose significant safety risks that can lead to serious injury or death for infants.

The baby loungers violate federal safety standards for infant sleep products, as their sides are too low to safely contain an infant. Additionally, the sleeping pad's thickness presents a suffocation risk, while the wider openings at the foot of the loungers create potential fall and entrapment hazards. The inclusion of crib bumpers, which are banned under federal law due to their suffocation risk, further exacerbates these dangers.
recalls.Title: Recall of LXDHSTRA Baby Loungers and Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation and Fall Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach LXDHSTRA via email at lxdhstrarecall@gmail.com.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2025 to August 2025, priced between $30 and $34.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation and fall hazards due to non-compliance with safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the loungers and bumpers, contact LXDHSTRA for a refund, and destroy the products as instructed.

recalls.metaDescription: LXDHSTRA recalls baby loungers and crib bumpers due to serious suffocation and fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: AUGNORYE padded crib bumpers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns, as they pose a risk of suffocation to infants. These bumpers come in sets of four, made from pink polyester, and are available in two sizes: approximately 27 inches by 9.6 inches and 52 inches by 9.6 inches. Each bumper features a care label with a unique series of letters and numbers stitched onto it.
recalls.Title: Recall of AUGNORYE Padded Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Risk; Violates Federal Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to YIXIA via email at Yixiakejirecall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled AUGNORYE Padded Cushioned Crib Bumpers
Care Label on Recalled Crib Bumper

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from February 2025 to May 2025, priced between $40 and $45.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshi Yixiakejiyouxiangongsi, dba YIXIA, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to obstruction of infant's breathing; violates the Safe Sleep for Babies Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the bumpers immediately and contact YIXIA for a full refund. They should remove the bumpers, cut the straps and padding, and send a photo of the destroyed items to Yixiakejirecall@163.com to receive their refund. YIXIA and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: AUGNORYE crib bumpers recalled due to suffocation risk. Contact YIXIA for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: The recall affects braided crib bumpers sold in various colors and lengths of 39, 59, 78, 118, and 158 inches. These bumpers are packaged in vacuum-sealed bags and lack any identifying labels. Due to their design, these products pose a serious suffocation hazard to infants, violating federal regulations against padded crib bumpers.
recalls.Title: Recall of Braided Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Risk; Federal Safety Violation; Available on Amazon from Haoyunm
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Haoyunm via email at hyzhongtshung@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Braided crib bumpers recalled due to suffocation risks. Stop using immediately and contact Haoyunm for a refund.

recalls.Description: Love Our Home has issued a recall for its braided crib bumpers due to serious safety concerns. These bumpers, available in lengths of one to four meters (3.28 to 13.12 feet), pose a suffocation hazard to infants and violate the federal ban on crib bumpers as mandated by the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Parents are urged to take immediate action for their children's safety.
recalls.Title: Love Our Home Braided Crib Bumpers Recalled for Suffocation Risk and Federal Compliance Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Love Our Home at loveourhomerecall@163.com.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Baofali 4-in-1 Microfiber Crib Safety Bumpers, which come in white, gray, and pink. These bumpers, measuring approximately 52 inches long and 10 inches tall, feature fabric ties and are rectangular in shape. They have model numbers JJ25B-A1, JJ25B-A2, and JJ25B-A3.
recalls.Title: Baofali Crib Bumpers Recalled for Safety Violations and Suffocation Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Baofali Trading via email at 2540535966@qq.com.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Temu.com from June 2024 to August 2024, priced between $25 and $30.

Distributors.Name: Baofali Trading Co. Ltd., dba Unique Person Home Goods, of China

Hazards.Name: These crib bumpers are in violation of the federal crib bumper ban, creating a suffocation risk for infants.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the crib bumpers immediately and remove them from cribs. To obtain a full refund, take a photo of the crib bumper cut in half and send it to 2540535966@qq.com or contact Baofali Trading on Temu.com. Dispose of the destroyed bumper according to local regulations. Baofali Trading will reach out to all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Baofali Crib Bumpers recalled due to suffocation risks. Immediate action required for refunds and safety.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for padded crib bumpers available in colors such as gray with white clouds, teddy bear, light pink, and white. These bumpers were packaged in a clear plastic bag and lack any identifying labels or markings. Infants are at risk of suffocation if they roll against the bumper in a way that blocks their breathing, and these products are prohibited under federal regulations.
recalls.Title: Padded Crib Bumpers Recalled for Suffocation Risk; Federal Ban Violation; Available on AliExpress
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can visit AliExpress online at https://customerservice.aliexpress.com/home?language=en&from=byr_common&hcMapRule=aeMapRule and select "online service" at the bottom of the page.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled crib bumpers immediately and reach out to AliExpress for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers should destroy the bumpers by cutting the straps and padding, then provide a photo as proof of destruction by replying to AliExpress's email. The company is reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.Description:
Zazaba International is recalling padded crib bumpers included in various crib bedding sets due to a serious suffocation hazard. These bumpers, which feature a variety of themes such as safari, astronaut, and elephant designs, were sold exclusively on Zazaba.com. The recall affects multiple sets, each identifiable by the tag that reads 'Grão de gente.' A detailed list of affected products is provided below.
Consumers are urged to stop using these crib bumpers immediately as they pose a risk to infants who may roll or become positioned in a way that obstructs their breathing. The sale of padded crib bumpers is prohibited under the Safe Sleep for Babies Act of 2021.
recalls.Title: Zazaba International Issues Recall for Grão de Gente Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Zazaba International Inc. at 855-425-4640 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at recall@graodegente.com. Additional details can be found online at https://graodegente.com/pages/crib-bumper-recall or by visiting https://graodegente.com and clicking on 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.
